_note: Hand-curated verse translations (translit + English meaning), keyed by song id. Each array aligns to the verse blocks of that song in order. Applied by translate.ts (review:verified). Grows incrementally, hymn by hymn.

dp_1.translit:
Minnaar thadamadhil soozh Villiputhoor endrorukaal
sonnaar kazharkamalam soodinom - munnaaL
kizhiyaruthaan endruraithom keezhmaiyinil serumva
zhiyaruthom nenjame vandhu
dp_1.meaning: Having once uttered the name of Villiputhur, girt by vast glittering ramparts, we have crowned ourselves with the lotus-feet of Periyalwar. We have declared him the one who long ago cut open the prize-purse in the Pandya court. O heart, by saying this we have severed the path that leads to baseness.

dp_1.translit:
Paandiyan kondaada Battarpiraan vandhaarendru
eendiya sangam eduthootha
vendiya vedangaL odhi viraindhu kizhi aruthaan
paadhangaL yaamudaiya patru
dp_1.meaning: As the Pandya king rejoiced and the gathered assembly blew their conches proclaiming that Battarpiran (Periyalwar) had come, he chanted the necessary Vedas and swiftly cut open the prize-purse, winning the debate. His feet alone are our refuge.

dp_1.translit:
Pallaandu pallaandu pallaayi raththaandu palakodi nooraayiram
mallaanda thiNthoL maNivaNNaa! un sevvadi sevvi thirukkaappu
dp_1.meaning: Many years, many years, many thousands of years, many millions and hundreds of thousands of years! O sapphire-hued Lord, whose strong shoulders vanquished the wrestlers — may auspicious protection rest upon your beautiful red feet.

dp_1.translit:
Adiyo modumnin nodum pirivindri aayiram pallaandu
vadivaai ninvala maarbinil vaazhginra mangaiyum pallaandu
vadivaar sodhi valaththurai yumsuda raazhiyum pallaandu
padaipor pukku muzhangumap paanchasanniyamum pallaande
dp_1.meaning: May we, your servants, live inseparably with you for a thousand years. May Lakshmi who dwells on your beautiful right chest live long; may the radiant discus on your right hand live long; and may the conch Panchajanya, which roars as it enters battle, live long.

dp_1.translit:
Vaazhaat pattunin reerullee rel vandhu maNNum maNamumkoNmin
koozhaat pattunin reergaLai engaL kuzhuvinil pugudhalottom
ezhaat kaalum pazhippilom naangaL iraakkadhar vaazh ilangai
paazhaa laagap padaiporu thaanukkup pallaandu kooruthame
dp_1.meaning: If you live truly devoted to him, come and receive the land and its fragrance — his service. But we will not admit into our fellowship those who serve merely for the sake of food. Blameless for seven generations, let us sing 'Pallandu' to the Lord who waged war and laid waste Lanka, the demons' abode.

dp_1.translit:
Edu nilaththil iduvadhan munnamvandhu engaL kuzhaampugundhu
koodu manamudai yeergaL varambozhi vandhuollaik koodumino
naadum nagaramum nankari yanamo naaraya naayavendru
paadu manamudaip paththarul leervandhu pallaandu koorumine
dp_1.meaning: Before the leaf of fate is cast upon the earth, come and enter our assembly. You who wish to join, throw off every limit and unite with us swiftly. So that the whole land and city may know, you devotees whose hearts long to sing 'Namo Narayanaya' — come and chant 'Pallandu'.

dp_1.translit:
ANdak kulaththuk kadhipathi yaagi asura riraakkadharai
iNdaik kulaththai eduthuk kaLaindha irudeeke santhanakku
thoNdak kulaththilul leervan thaditozhudhu aayira naamamsolli
paNdaik kulaththaith thavirndhupal laandupal laayirath thaaNdenmine
dp_1.meaning: To Hrishikesha, lord of all the worlds, who uprooted and cast away the clustered hordes of asuras and demons — you who belong to the lineage of servitude, come, worship his feet, recite his thousand names, cast off your old lowly lineage, and proclaim, 'Many years, many thousands of years!'

dp_1.translit:
Endhai thandhai thandhaitham mooththappan ezhpadi kaalthodangi
vandhu vazhivazhi aatcheyhin romthiru voNath thiruvizhavil
andhiyam podhi lariyuru vaagi ariyai yazhiththavanai
pandhanai theerappal laandupal laayirath thaandendru paaduthame
dp_1.meaning: From our father, grandfather, great-grandfather, and back through seven generations, we have served him in unbroken succession. On the sacred Sravana (Thiruvonam) festival, that our bondage may end, let us sing 'Many years, many thousands of years!' to him who, at dusk, took the form of the man-lion and destroyed the foe Hiranya.

dp_1.translit:
Theeyir poliginra senjuda raazhi thigazhthiruch chakkaraththin
koyir poriyaale otruNdu nindru kudikudi aatcheyhinrom
maayap porupadai vaaNanai aayiran thoLum pozhiguruthi
paayach chuzhatriya aazhival laanukkup pallaandu kooruthume
dp_1.meaning: Sealed with the mark of the sacred discus that gleams like blazing fire, we stand and serve him household by household. Let us sing 'Pallandu' to the mighty wielder of the discus, who whirled it so that the thousand arms of Banasura poured forth blood.

dp_1.translit:
Neyyidai nallathor sorum niyadhamum aththaaNich chevagamum
kaiyadaik kaayum kazhuththukkup pooNodu kaadhukkuk kuNdalamum
meyyida nallathor saandhamum thanduennai veLLuyi raakkavalla
paiyudai naagap pagaikkodi yaanukkup pallaandu kooruvane
dp_1.meaning: To him who bears the Garuda banner — the foe of the hooded serpents — who gave me fine ghee-rice, constant intimate service, betel for the hand, ornaments for the neck, earrings for the ears, and fragrant sandal-paste for the body, and who made my soul pure: to him I will sing 'Pallandu'.

dp_1.translit:
Uduththuk kalaindhanin peethaga vaadai yuduthuk kalaththadhuNdu
thoduththa thuzhaaymalar soodik kalaindhana soodumith thoNdargaLom
viduththa thisaikkaru mamthiruth thiththiru voNath thiruvizhavil
paduththapain naagaNaip paLLikoN daanukkup pallaandu kooruthume
dp_1.meaning: We servants wear the golden silk you have worn and laid aside, eat the food left in your vessel, and adorn ourselves with the tulasi garlands you have worn and removed. Carrying out the duties you assign in every direction, on the sacred Sravana festival let us sing 'Pallandu' to him who reclines upon the fresh-hooded serpent Adisesha.

dp_1.translit:
EnnaaL emperu maanun thanakkadi yomendru ezhuththuppatta
annaaLe adiyongaL adikkudil veedupetru uyndhadhu kaaN
sennaaL thotrith thirumadhu raiyuL silai kuniththu aindhalaiya
painnaa gaththalai paayndhava ne unnaip pallaandu kooruthume
dp_1.meaning: O our Lord, the very day we were enrolled as your servants, on that very day our humble households were liberated and saved — behold! O you who appeared on an auspicious day, bent the bow in sacred Mathura, and leapt upon the five-hooded serpent Kaliya — to you we sing 'Pallandu'.

dp_1.translit:
Alvazhak kondrumil laaaNi kottiyar konabhi maanathungan
selvanaip polath thirumaa lenaanum unakkup pazhavadiyen
nalvagai yaalnamo naaraa yaNaavendru naamam palaparavi
palvagaiyaalum pavithira neunnaip pallaandu kooruvane
dp_1.meaning: Like the noble lord of Kottiyur — free of all unrighteousness, proud only in his devotion — O Thirumal, I too am your age-old servant. Rightly reciting your many names and saying 'Namo Narayanaya', in every way, O purifier, I will sing 'Pallandu' to you.

dp_1.translit:
PallaaN dendru pavithira naipparamettiyai saarngamennum
villaaN daanthannai villiputhoorvittu siththan virumbiyasol
nallaaN dendru navindruraip paarnamo naaraa yaNaayavendru
pallaaN dumparamaathmanaich choozhndhirun dheththuvar pallaande
dp_1.meaning: These are the loving words of Vishnuchittan (Periyalwar) of Villiputhur, singing 'Pallandu' to the pure One, the Supreme, the wielder of the Sarnga bow. Those who recite them well as an auspicious hymn, saying 'Namo Narayanaya', shall forever surround the Supreme Self and worship him for endless years.

dp_2.translit:
VaNNa maadangaL soozh thirukkottiyoor
kaNNan kesavan nambi piranthinil
eNNey suNNam ethirethir thoovida
kaNNan mutram kalandhu aLaraayitre
dp_2.meaning: When Lord Kannan (Krishna), Kesava, was born in Thirukkottiyur ringed by colourful mansions, the people threw oil and fragrant powder upon one another in joy, until Kannan's courtyard, all churned and trampled, turned to slush.

dp_2.translit:
Oduvaar vizhuvaar ugandhu aalippaar
naaduvaar nambiraan engutraan enbaar
paaduvaargaLum palparai kotta nindru
aaduvaargaLum aayitru aaypaadiye
dp_2.meaning: Some run, some fall, some shout for joy; some search about, asking 'Where is our Lord?'. With many drums beating, some stand and sing while others dance — such has Gokulam, the cowherds' village, become.

dp_2.translit:
Penich cheerudaip piLLai piranthinil
kaaNath thaam puguvaar pukkup podhuvaar
aaNoppaar ivan nerillai kaaN thiru
voNaththaan ulagaaLum enbaargaLe
dp_2.meaning: At the birth of this cherished, glorious child, people throng in to see him and then stream out again. 'There is no man to equal him; behold, none is his match,' they say — 'this child born under the Sravana star shall rule the world.'

dp_2.translit:
Uriyai mutraththu uruttinindru aaduvaar
naRuney paal thayir nandraagath thoovuvaar
seRimen koondhal avizhath thiLaiththu engum
aRivazhindhanar aaypaadi aayare
dp_2.meaning: They roll the hanging pot-slings across the courtyard and dance; they sprinkle fragrant ghee, milk and curd in abundance; their thick soft tresses loosen as they revel — everywhere the cowherds of Gokulam lose themselves, carried beyond their senses with joy.

dp_2.translit:
KoNda thaaL uri kolak kodumazhu
thaNdinar paRi olaich chayanaththar
viNda mullai arumbanna pallinar
aNdar miNdip pugundhu ney aadinaar
dp_2.meaning: Bearing curved staffs and looped slings, carrying axes and clubs, with their rough palm-leaf mats and teeth like budding jasmine, the cowherds came crowding in and bathed one another in ghee.

dp_2.translit:
Kaiyum kaalum nimirthuk kadaaraneer
paiya aattip pasunjiRu manjaLaal
aiya naa vazhithaaLukku angaandhida
vaiyam ezhum kaNdaaL piLLai vaayuLe
dp_2.meaning: Straightening his little hands and feet and bathing him gently in warm water from the cauldron, Yashoda cleansed his tongue with fresh tender turmeric; and when the babe opened his mouth wide, she beheld all seven worlds within it.

dp_2.translit:
Vaayul vaiyagam kaNda mada nallaar
aayar puthiran allan arun theyvam
paaya seerudaip paNbudaip baalakan
maayan endru magizhndhanar maathare
dp_2.meaning: The gentle women who saw the worlds within his mouth rejoiced, saying: 'He is no mere cowherd's son, but the rare Supreme God — this child of boundless glory and grace is the wondrous Mayan (Vishnu) himself.'

dp_2.translit:
Paththu naaLum kadandha irandaa naaL
eththisaiyum sayamaram kodiththu
maththa maa malai thaangiya maindhanai
uththaanam seydhu ugandhanar aayare
dp_2.meaning: On the twelfth day, the ten days having passed, the cowherds raised festive victory-poles and banners in every direction, and joyfully performed the cradle rite for the mighty child who would one day lift the great Govardhana mountain.

dp_2.translit:
Kidakkil thottil kizhiya uthaiththidum
eduthuk koLLil marungai iRuththidum
odukkip pulgil udharaththe paaindhidum
midukku ilaamaiyaal naan melindhen nangaai
dp_2.meaning: When laid down he kicks hard enough to tear the cradle; when picked up he wrenches at one's waist; when held close he lunges at the belly — O dear lady, for want of strength to manage him, I have quite worn myself out!

dp_2.translit:
Sennel aar vayal soozh thirukkottiyoor
mannu naaraNan nambi piranthamai
minnu nool vittusiththan viritha ip
pannu paadal vallaarkku illai paavame
dp_2.meaning: Thus has Vishnuchittan (Periyalwar), wearer of the shining sacred thread, sung in full of the birth of the eternal Lord Narayana at Thirukkottiyur, girt by fields of ripe red paddy. For those who can master these well-wrought verses, there shall be no sin.

dp_2.translit:
Seethak kadaluL amudhanna devaki
kodhaik kuzhalaaL asodhaikkup poththandha
pedhaik kuzhavi pidiththuch chuvaiththuNNum
paadhak kamalangaL kaaNeere pavaLavaayeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: The innocent babe whom Devaki — nectar-like, risen from the cool ocean — handed over to the garland-tressed Yashoda: behold the lotus feet that this child grasps and sucks with relish! O coral-lipped women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Muththum maNiyum vayiramum nanponnum
thaththip padhiththuth thalaippeydhaaR pol engum
paththu viralum maNivaNNan paadhangaL
oththittu irundhavaa kaaNeere oNNudhaleer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: Like pearls, gems, diamonds and fine gold scattered and inlaid together everywhere — behold how the ten toes and the feet of the sapphire-hued one are matched in beauty! O bright-browed women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
PaNaiththoL iLavaaychchi paal paaindha kongai
aNaiththaara uNdu kidandha ip piLLai
iNaikkaalil veLLith thaLai nindru ilangum
kaNaikkaal irundhavaa kaaNeere kaarigaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: This child, who lay nestling and drinking deep at the milk-flowing breast of the strong-shouldered young cowherd-mother — behold how his rounded calves are, with silver anklets shining on his paired feet! O lovely women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Uzhandhaal naRuney orodhadaa vuNNa
izhandhaaL erivinaal eerththu ezhil maththin
pazhandhaambaal ochchap payaththaal thavazhndhaan
muzhandhaaL irundhavaa kaaNeere mugizhmulaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: When Yashoda, who had toiled at the churn, lost her temper because he ate up a whole pot of fragrant ghee, and brandished at him the old rope of the lovely churn — behold the knees on which he crawled away in fear! O budding-breasted women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
PiRangiya peychchi mulaisuvaith thuNdittu
uRanguvaan pole kidandha ip piLLai
maRangoL iraNiyan maarbaimun keeNdaan
kuRangu kaLaivandhu kaaNeere kuvimulaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: This child, who sucked dry the breast of the splendid demoness (Putana) and then lay as if asleep — he who long ago tore open the chest of the wrathful Hiranya — behold his little thighs! O firm-breasted women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Maththak kaLitru vasudevar thammudai
siththam piriyaadha devaki than vayitril
aththaththin paththaa naaL thondriya achchudhan
muththam irundhavaa kaaNeere mugizhnagaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: Achyuta, who appeared on the tenth day of the asterism Hasta in the womb of Devaki — she who never parted from the heart of Vasudeva, lord of rutting elephants — behold his little pearl-teeth! O bud-smiling women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Irungai madhakaLiru eerkkindra avanai
parungip paRiththukkoNdu odu paraman than
nerungu pavaLamum nernaaNum muththum
marungum irundhavaa kaaNeere vaaNudhaleer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: The Supreme One who snatched away and ran off with the calf from the great rutting tusker that was dragging it — behold his waist, set with close-strung coral, the trim waist-cord, and pearls! O bright-browed women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Vandha madhalaik kuzhaaththai valiseydhu
thandhak kaLirupol thaane viLaiyaadum
nandhan madhalaikku nandru mazhagiya
undhi irundhavaa kaaNeere oLiyizhaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: Nanda's little son, who bullies the band of children that come, and then plays on by himself like a tusked elephant — behold how very lovely his navel is! O bright-jewelled women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Adhirum kadalniRa vaNNanai aaychchi
madhura mulaiyootti vanjiththu vaiththu
padhaRap padaame pazhandhaambaal aarththa
udharam irundhavaa kaaNeere oLivaLaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: The cowherd-mother, having coaxed the roaring-ocean-hued one by suckling him with sweet milk, then bound him with the old churning-rope so he might not struggle free: behold his belly so bound! O bright-bangled women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Perumaa uralil piNippuNdu irundhuangu
irumaa marudham iRuththa ip piLLai
gurumaa maNippooN kulaavith thigazhum
thirumaarbu irundhavaa kaaNeere seyizhaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: This child, who though bound to the great mortar broke down the two huge marudam trees — behold his sacred chest, resplendent and graced with great lustrous jewelled ornaments! O fine-jewelled women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
NaaLgaLor naalaindhu thingaL aLavile
thaaLai nimirththuch chakadaththaich chaadippoy
vaaLkoL vaLaiyeyitru aaruyir vavvinaan
thoLgaL irundhavaa kaaNeere surikuzhaleer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: Within just a few months, he stretched out his foot and kicked over the cart, and seized away the precious life of the demoness of sword-sharp curved fangs (Putana) — behold his shoulders! O curly-tressed women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Maiththadang kaNNi yasodhai vaLarkkindra
seyththalai neela niRaththuch chiRuppiLLai
neyththalai nemiyum sangum nilaaviya
kaiththalangaL vandhu kaaNeere kanankuzhaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: The little dark-blue-hued child whom wide-collyrium-eyed Yashoda rears — behold his palms, on which gleam the oil-bright discus and the conch! O heavy-earringed women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
VaNdamar poonguzhal aaychchi maganaagak
koNdu vaLarkkindra kovalak kuttaRku
aNdamum naadum adanga vizhungiya
kaNdam irundhavaa kaaNeere kaarigaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: To the little cowherd-boy whom the bee-swarmed, flower-tressed cowherd-mother takes for her own son and rears — behold the throat that swallowed up the whole cosmos and all the worlds! O lovely women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Endhondai vaaychchingam vaavendreduthukkoNdu
andhondai vaayamu dhaadhariththu aaychchiyar
thamdhondai vaayaal tharukkip parugum ich
chendhondai vaay vandhu kaaNeere seyizhaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: 'Come, my coral-lipped lion-cub!' — so saying, the cowherd-women take him up and, cherishing the nectar of his red kovai-fruit lips, drink it proudly with their own lips: behold this red coral mouth! O fine-jewelled women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Nokki yasodhai nuNukkiya manjaLaal
naakku vazhiththu neeraattum in nambikku
vaakkum nayanamum vaayum muRuvalum
mookkum irundhavaa kaaNeere moykuzhaleer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: To this perfect child whom Yashoda watches over, cleansing his tongue with fine turmeric as she bathes him — behold his speech, his eyes, his mouth, his smile, and his nose! O thick-tressed women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
ViNkoL amarargaL vedhanai theeramun
maNkoL vasudevar thammaganaay vandhu
thiNkoL asurarai theya vaLargindraan
kaNgaL irundhavaa kaaNeere kanavaLaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: He who, to end the anguish of the sky-dwelling celestials, came as the son of earth-born Vasudeva and grows up to waste away the mighty asuras — behold his eyes! O dense-bangled women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Paruvam nirambaame paarellaam uyya
thiruvin vadivokkum devaki petra
uruvu kariya oLimaNi vaNNan
puruvam irundhavaa kaaNeere pooNmulaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: The dark-formed, lustrous-gem-hued one whom Devaki — fair as Lakshmi's own form — bore, though not yet of full age, that all the earth might be saved — behold his eyebrows! O ornament-breasted women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
MaNNum malaiyum kadalum ulagezhum
uNNun thiRaththu magizhndhuNNum piLLaikku
vaNNam ezhilkoL magarak kuzhaiyivai
thiNNam irundhavaa kaaNeere seyizhaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: To the child who gladly swallows the earth, the mountains, the seas and all seven worlds — behold these beautifully-hued makara (fish-shaped) ear-ornaments, set firm! O fine-jewelled women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Muttrilum thoodhaiyum munkaimmel poovaiyum
sittril izhaiththuth thiritharu vorgaLai
patrip paRiththukkoNdu odum paraman than
netri irundhavaa kaaNeere nerizhaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: The Supreme One who snatches and runs off with the little baskets, the toy pots, and the mynah-birds from the forearms of the children who wander building toy houses — behold his forehead! O fine-jewelled women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Azhagiya paimponnin kolangaik koNdu
kazhalgaL sadhangai kalandhu engu maarppa
mazhakandrinangaL maRiththuth thirivaan
kuzhalgaL irundhavaa kaaNeere kuvimulaiyeer vandhu kaaNeere
dp_2.meaning: Taking a lovely staff of fresh gold in his hand, his anklets and little bells jingling all around, he roams turning back the herds of young calves — behold how his curling locks are! O firm-breasted women, come and behold!

dp_2.translit:
Suruppaar kuzhali yasodhai munsonna
thiruppaadha kesaththaith thenputhuvaip pattan
viruppaal uraiththa irubadhodu ondrum
uraippaarpoy vaikundhath ondruvar thaame
dp_2.meaning: These twenty-one verses, lovingly composed by the lord of southern Puduvai (Periyalwar) on the sacred form from foot to hair that bee-tressed Yashoda first described — those who recite them shall go and unite with Vaikuntha.

dp_2.translit:
MaaNikkam katti vayiram idaikatti
aaNippon naalseydha vaNNach chiRuththottil
peNi unakkup biraman vidudhandhaan
maaNik kuRaLane thaalelo! Vaiyam aLandhaane thaalelo!
dp_2.meaning: Set with rubies and bordered with diamonds, a pretty little coloured cradle wrought of pure gold — lovingly Brahma has sent it for you. O gem-like dwarf (Vamana), thaalelo (sleep, my child)! O you who measured the worlds, thaalelo!

dp_2.translit:
Udaiyaar kanamaNiyodu oNmaa thuLampoo
idaiviravik koththa ezhilthezhki nodu
vidaiyeRu kaabaali eesan vidudhandhaan;
udaiyaay azhel azhel thaalelo! Ulagam aLandhaane thaalelo!
dp_2.meaning: A lovely waist-band of rich heavy gems strung interspersed with bright pomegranate-flowers — Lord Shiva, the bull-riding Kapali, has sent it. O my master, weep not, weep not, thaalelo! O you who measured the worlds, thaalelo!

dp_2.translit:
Endham piraanaar ezhilthiru maarvarkku
sandha mazhagiya thaamaraith thaaLarkku,
indhiran thaanum ezhiludaik kiNkiNi
thandhu uvanaay nindraan thaalelo! Thaamaraik kaNNane thaalelo!
dp_2.meaning: For my Lord of the beautiful sacred chest, with feet graceful as lovely lotuses, Indra himself has given beautiful jingling anklets and stands by in service. Thaalelo! O lotus-eyed one, thaalelo!

dp_2.translit:
Sangin valampuriyum sevadik kiNkiNiyum
angaich charivaLaiyum naaNum araiththodarum
angaN visumbil amarargaL poththandhaar,
sengaN karumugile! Thaalelo! Devaki singame thaalelo!
dp_2.meaning: A right-spiralled conch, anklets for the red feet, bangles for the lovely hands, a waist-cord and a waist-chain — the celestials of the wide bright sky have brought these. O red-eyed dark cloud, thaalelo! O lion-cub of Devaki, thaalelo!

dp_2.translit:
Ezhilaar thirumaarvukku eRku mivaiyendru
azhagiya aimpadaiyum aaramum koNdu
vazhuvil kodaiyaan vaichchiravaNan
thozhudhu uvanaay nindraan thaalelo! Thoomani vaNNane thaalelo!
dp_2.meaning: 'These befit his beautiful sacred chest' — so saying, Kubera (Vaishravana) of flawless generosity, bearing a lovely five-weapons amulet and a garland, worships and stands by. Thaalelo! O pure-sapphire-hued one, thaalelo!

dp_2.translit:
Odhak kadalin oLimuththi naaramum
saadhip pavaLamum sandhach charivaLaiyum
maadhakka vendru varuNan vidudhandhaan
sodhich chudarmudiyaay thaalelo! Sundharath thoLane thaalelo!
dp_2.meaning: A garland of lustrous pearls from the surging sea, fine coral, and graceful bangles — 'let these adorn him,' said Varuna, and sent them. O you of the radiant gleaming crown, thaalelo! O one of beautiful shoulders, thaalelo!

dp_2.translit:
Kaanaar naRundhuzhaay kaiseydha kaNNiyum
vaanaar sezhunjolaik kaRpagaththin vaasigaiyum
thenaar malarmel thirumangai poththandhaaL
kone! Azhel azhel thaalelo! Kudandhaik kidandhaane thaalelo!
dp_2.meaning: A garland crafted of fragrant forest-tulasi, and a wreath from the karpaka tree of the rich heavenly groves — Lakshmi, who dwells on the honeyed lotus, has brought them. O king, weep not, weep not, thaalelo! O you who recline at Kudanthai (Kumbakonam), thaalelo!

dp_2.translit:
Kachchodu poRsurikai kaambu kanakavaLai
uchchi maNichchutti oNthaaL niraip poRpoo
achchudhanukku endru avaniyaaL poththandhaaL
nachchumulai uNdaay thaalelo naaraayaNaa azhel thaalelo!
dp_2.meaning: A bodice, a golden dagger with its haft, gold bangles, a jewelled head-ornament, and a row of bright golden flowers for the feet — 'these are for Achyuta,' said the Earth-goddess, and brought them. O you who drank the poison-breast of Putana, thaalelo! O Narayana, weep not, thaalelo!

dp_2.translit:
Meythimirum naanap podiyodu manjaLum
seyya thadangaNNukku anjanamum sindhuramum
veyya kalaippaagi koNdu uvaLaay nindraaL
ayyaa azhel azhel thaalelo! Arangath aNaiyaane thaalelo!
dp_2.meaning: Fragrant musk-powder that thrills the body, and turmeric, kohl for the lovely wide eyes, and vermilion — the goddess of the fierce arts bears these and stands by. O sir, weep not, weep not, thaalelo! O you who recline at Srirangam, thaalelo!

dp_2.translit:
Vanjanaiyaal vandha peychchi mulai uNda
anjana vaNNanai aaychchi thaalaattiya
senjol maRaiyavar ser puthuvaip pattan sol
enjaamai vallavarkku illai idarthaane
dp_2.meaning: These are the words of the lord of Puduvai — home of the Vedic brahmins of true speech — on Yashoda's lullaby to the collyrium-hued one who drank the breast of the deceitful demoness Putana. For those who can master them without omission, there shall be no sorrow.

dp_2.translit:
Thanmugath thuchchutti thoongath thoongath thavazhndhupoy
ponmugak kiNkiNi yaarppap puzhudhi yaLaigindraan
enmagan govindhan kooththinai iLa maamadhee
ninmugam kaNNuLa vaagil nee inge nokkippo
dp_2.meaning: With the jewel-pendant on his brow swinging to and fro, he crawls along, his golden anklets ringing, and plays in the dust. O young great moon, if you have eyes in your face to see, look this way and behold the dance of my son Govinda before you pass on!

dp_2.translit:
EnsiRuk kuttan enako rinnamudhu empiraan
thansiRuk kaigaLaal kaattik kaatti yazhaikkindran
anjanavaNNanodu aadalaada uRudhiyel
manjil maRaiyaadhe maamadhee magizhndhodivaa
dp_2.meaning: My little one — my own sweet nectar, my Lord — beckons you again and again with his little hands. O great moon, if you mean to come and play with the collyrium-hued one, do not hide behind the clouds; come running, gladly!

dp_2.translit:
Suttrum olivattam soozhndhu sodhi parandhengum
eththanai seyyilum enmagan mugam nerovvaay
viththagan vengada vaaNan unnai viLikkindra
kaiththalam novaame ambuli kadi thodivaa
dp_2.meaning: Though a halo of light surrounds you and your radiance spreads everywhere, do whatever you may, you can never match my son's face. The wondrous Lord of Venkatam is calling you — O moon, come quickly, lest his little hand ache from beckoning!

dp_2.translit:
Sakkarak kaiyan thadangaNNaal malara vizhiththu
okkalai melirundhu unnaiye suttik kaattumkaaN
thakka thaRidhiyel sandhiraa salam seyyaadhe
makkat peRaadha malada nallaiyel vaakaNdaay
dp_2.meaning: The discus-handed one, opening wide his lovely eyes, seated on his mother's hip, keeps pointing you out. O moon, if you have any sense, play no tricks; unless you are as barren as one who bears no children, come!

dp_2.translit:
Azhagiya vaayil amudha ooral theLivuRa
mazhalai muttraadha iLanjollaal unnaik koogindraan
kuzhagan sireedharan koovak koova nee podhiyel
puzhaiyila aagaadhe ninsevi pugar maa madhee
dp_2.meaning: From his lovely mouth, the clear nectar welling up, in lisping baby-words not yet fully formed he calls you. O bright great moon, if you go away even as the playful Sridhara calls and calls, your ears will prove to be of no use!

dp_2.translit:
ThaNdodu sakkaram saarngamendhum thadakkaiyan
kaNthuyil koLLakkarudhik kottaavi koLgindraan
uNda mulaippaal aRaakaNdaay uRangaavidil
viNdanil manniya maamadhee virain thodivaa
dp_2.meaning: The broad-handed one who bears mace, discus and the Sarnga bow, now minded to sleep, is yawning. See — the breast-milk he drank is not yet digested; if he does not sleep he will be unwell. O great moon set firm in the sky, come swiftly!

dp_2.translit:
Baalakan endru paribavam seyyel paNdu or naaL
aalin ilai vaLarndha siRukkan avan ivan
mel ezhap paayndhu pidiththukkoLLum veguLumel
maalai madhiyaadhe maa madhee magizhndhu odi vaa
dp_2.meaning: Do not slight him as a mere child: this is that very little one who long ago lay floating on a banyan leaf. If he is angered, he will spring up and seize hold of you. O great moon, do not disregard Tirumal — come gladly, running!

dp_2.translit:
SiRiyanendru enniLanj chingaththai igazhelkaNdaay
siRumaiyin vaarththaiyai maavaliyidaich chendrukeL
siRumaip pizhaikoLLil neeyum un thevaikkuriyaikaaN
niRaima dhee nedu maalviraindhu unnaik koovugindran
dp_2.meaning: Do not despise my young lion as 'a small one' — see! Go and ask Mahabali about the matter of his smallness (how the dwarf grew to span the worlds). If you commit the error of scorning his smallness, you too will answer to your own deity. O full moon, Tirumal is calling you in haste!

dp_2.translit:
Thaazhiyil veNNey thadangai aara vizhungiya
pezhai vayitru empiraan kaNdaay unnaik koogindraan
aazhikoNdu unnai eRiyum aiyuRavu illai kaaN
vaazha uRudhiyel maa madhee magizhndhu odi vaa
dp_2.meaning: Behold — my Lord of the capacious belly, who swallowed pots of butter by the handful, is calling you. There is no doubt: he may fling his discus at you if you delay. O great moon, if you wish to live, come gladly, running!

dp_2.translit:
Maiththadang kaNNi yasodhai thanmaganukku ivai
oththana solli uraiththa maatram oLipuththoor
viththagan vittusiththan viriththa thamizh ivai
eththanaiyum solla vallavarkku idar illaiye
dp_2.meaning: These are the fitting words wide-collyrium-eyed Yashoda spoke to her son — the Tamil verses elaborated by the wise Vishnuchittan (Periyalwar) of radiant Puduvai. For those who can recite even some of them, there shall be no distress.

dp_2.translit:
Uyya ulagupadaith thuNda maNivayiraa
oozhithor oozhipala aalin ilaiyadhanmel
paiya uyogudhuyil koNda paramparane
pangaya neeLnayanaththu anjana meniyane
seyyavaL ninnagalam semame nakkarudhich
chelvupo limagarak kaadhu thigazhndhilaga
aiyaena kkorukaal aaduka sengeerai
aayargaL poreRe aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: O gem-bellied one who created the worlds for their salvation and then swallowed them; Supreme of the supreme, who through aeon after aeon gently took your yogic sleep upon a banyan leaf; O collyrium-hued one of long lotus-eyes — Lakshmi rests upon your chest, deeming it her refuge, while the fish-shaped earrings of fortune gleam radiant. O father, for me just once do the sengeerai (baby's sway-dance); O bull among the cowherds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
KoLari yinnuruvang koNdu avuNanudalam
kurudhi kuzhambiyezhak koorugi raalkudaivaay
meeLa avanmaganai meymmai koLakkarudhi
melai yamararpadhi mikku veguNduvara
kaaLanan megamavai kallodu kaalpozhiyak
karudhi varaikkudaiyaak kaaligaL kaappavane
aaLaena kkorukaal aaduka sengeerai
aayargaL poreRe aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: O you who, taking the form of the fierce man-lion, scooped open the demon Hiranya's body with sharp claws till his blood gushed and clotted; and who, minded to make his son Prahlada truly your own — when the enraged king of the celestials (Indra) came and the dark clouds rained stones and wind — raised the mountain as an umbrella and shielded the cattle. To make me your servant, for me just once do the sengeerai; O bull among the cowherds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
Nammudai naayagane naanmaRai yinporuLe
naabiyuL naRkamala naanmuga nukkorukaal
thammanai yaanavane tharaNi thalamuzhudhum
thaaragai yinnulagum thadavi adhanpuRamum
vimma vaLarndhavane vezhamum ezhvidaiyum
viraviya velaidhanuL vendru varumavane
ammaena kkorukaal aaduka sengeerai
aayargaL poreRe aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: O our Lord, very meaning of the four Vedas; you who were once like a mother to four-faced Brahma, born from the good lotus in your navel; you who grew vast, spanning all the earth, the starry worlds and beyond; you who came victorious — taming the elephant and the seven bulls — from the dense ocean. O dear one, for me just once do the sengeerai; O bull among the cowherds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
Vaanavar thaammagizha vansaka damuruLa
vanjamu laippeyin nanjamadhu uNdavane
kaanaka valviLavin kaayudhi rakkarudhik
kandradhu koNdeRiyum karuniRa enkandre
thenuka num muranum thiNthiRal vennarakan
enbavar thaammadiyach cheruvadha rachchellum
aanai enakkorukaal aaduka sengeerai
aayargaL poreRe aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: O you who, that the celestials might rejoice, made the cruel cart (Sakatasura) roll over and drank the poison of the deceitful breast-demoness Putana; my dark-hued calf, who flung the calf-demon to bring down the fruits of the wild wood-apple tree; O elephant among heroes who go into battle so that Dhenuka, Mura and the fiercely strong vile Naraka fall dead — for me just once do the sengeerai; O bull among the cowherds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
Maththala vumthayirum vaarkuzhal nanmadavaar
vaiththana neykaLavaal vaarivizhungi orungu
oththa iNaimarudham unniya vandhavarai
ooru karaththinodum undhiya vendhiraloy
muththini LamuRuval mutra varuvadhanmun
munna mugaththaNiyaar moykuzhalgaL alaiya
aththa enakkorukaal aaduka sengeerai
aayargaL poreRe aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: O you of fierce might, who by stealth gulped down the churned butter and curd the fine long-tressed women had set aside, and who, when tied to the mortar, pushed apart with your hip the two matched marudam trees that meant to fall on you — before your young pearl-smile fully blooms, while the fragrant thick curls dangle over your face, O father, for me just once do the sengeerai; O bull among the cowherds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
Kaaya malarniRavaa karumugil poluruvaa
kaanaka maamaduvil kaaliya nuchchiyile
thooya nadampayilum sundhara ensiRuvaa
thunga madhakkariyin kombu paRiththavane
aaya maRindhuporu vaanedhir vandhamallai
andhara mindriyazhith thaadiya thaaLiNaiyaay
aaya enakkorukaal aaduka sengeerai
aayargaL poreRe aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: O you hued like the kaaya blossom, your form like a dark cloud; my handsome little one who danced your pure dance upon the head of Kaliya in the great forest pool; you who plucked off the tusk of the lofty rutting elephant; O you whose paired feet, knowing their craft, destroyed without respite the wrestlers who came to fight — for me just once do the sengeerai; O bull among the cowherds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
Thuppudai yayaargaLtham solvazhu vaadhuorukaal
thooya karunguzhalnal thogai mayilanaiya
nappinai thanthiRamaa nalvidai yezhaviya
nalla thiraludaiya naadhanum aanavane
thappina piLLaigaLaith thanamigu sodhipugath
thaniyoru therkadavith thaayodu koottiyaen
appa enakkorukaal aaduka sengeerai
aayargaL poreRe aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: O you who, at the cowherds' word, became the mighty lord by subduing without fail the seven fine bulls for the sake of Nappinnai — pure dark-tressed, lovely as a rich-plumed peacock; my father, who drove a single chariot to bring back the lost children (Sandipani's sons) into the supreme effulgence and restore them to their mother — for me just once do the sengeerai; O bull among the cowherds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
Unnaiyum okkalaiyil koNdu thamilmaruvi
unnodu thangaLkaruththaayina seydhuvarum
kanniya rummagizhak kaNdavar kaNkuLirak
katravar thetrivarap petra enakkaruLi
mannu kurungudiyaay veLLaRai yaay madhiLsoozh
solai malaikkarase kaNNa puraththamudhe
ennavalam kaLaivaay aaduka sengeerai
ezhulagum mudaiyaay aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: O Lord enshrined at lasting Kurungudi, at Thiruvellarai, king of rampart-ringed Azhagarmalai, nectar of Thirukkannapuram — gracing me, the mother who bore you, while the maidens take you on their hips and dote as they please, while onlookers' eyes are cooled and the learned throng to see — relieve my sorrow and do the sengeerai; O you who own the seven worlds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
Paalodu neythayiroN saandhodu saNpagamum
pangayam nallakarup pooramum naaRivara
kola naRumpavaLach chendhuvar vaayinidaik
komaLa veLLimuLaip polsila pallilaga
neela niRaththazhagaa raimpadai yinnaduve
ninkani vaayamudham itru muRindhuvizha
elu maRaipporuLe aaduka sengeerai
ezhulagum mudaiyaay aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: While milk, ghee, curd, fine sandal, champak, lotus and good camphor breathe their fragrance about you; while a few teeth like tender silver shoots gleam within your lovely fragrant-coral red mouth; O beautiful blue-hued one, amid the five weapons, as the nectar of your ripe-fruit lips spills over — O fitting meaning of the Veda, do the sengeerai; O you who own the seven worlds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
Sengama lakkazhalil sittridhazh polviralil
serthiga zhaazhigaLum kiNkiNi yumaraiyil
thangiya ponvadamum thaaLanan maadhuLaiyin
poovodu ponmaNiyum modhira mumkiRiyum
mangala aimpadaiyum tholvaLai yumkuzhaiyum
makaramum vaaLigaLum suttiyum oththilaga
engaL kudikkarase aaduka sengeerai
ezhulagum mudaiyaay aaduka aadukave
dp_2.meaning: On the red-lotus feet and the toes like little petals, gleaming rings and jingling anklets; at the waist a golden cord; a pomegranate-flower ornament and golden gem at the feet; rings and a toy-rattle; the auspicious five-weapons amulet; arm-bands and earrings; the fish-ornaments, the bright crescent-jewels and the head-pendant all shining together — O king of our clan, do the sengeerai; O you who own the seven worlds, dance, dance!

dp_2.translit:
Annamum meenuruvum aaLari yumkuRaLum
aamaiyu maanavane aayargaL naayagane
enava lamkaLaivaay aaduka sengeerai
ezhulagum mudaiyaay aaduka vaadukavendru
anna nadaimadavaaL asodhai yugandhaparisu
aana pugazhpputhuvaip patta nuraiththathamizh
innisai maalaigaLip paththumval laarulagil
eNdisaiyum pugazhmikku inbam adhu eydhuvare
dp_2.meaning: 'O you who became the swan, the fish, the man-lion, the dwarf and the tortoise; O chief of the cowherds — relieve my sorrow and do the sengeerai; O you who own the seven worlds, dance, dance!' — so swan-gaited Yashoda rejoiced. Those who master these ten garlands of sweet Tamil music, composed by the renowned lord of Puduvai, shall win fame in all eight directions and attain that bliss.

dp_2.translit:
MaaNikkak kiNkiNi yaarppa marunginmel
aaNippon naalseydha aaypon nudaimaNi
peNip pavaLavaay muththilanga paNdu
kaaNikoNda kaigaLaal sappaani
karunguzhal kuttane sappaani
dp_2.meaning: While the ruby anklets jingle and the choice jewels set in pure gold gleam at your waist, and pearls shine cherished in your coral mouth — clap your hands, the hands that once claimed and measured the worlds! Clap, O dark-curled little one, clap your hands (sappani)!

dp_2.translit:
Ponnarai naaNodu maaNikkak kiNkiNi
thannarai yaadath thanichchutti thaazhndhaada
ennarai melnin rizhindhuungaL aayartham
mannarai melkottaay sappaani
maayavane kottaay sappaani
dp_2.meaning: As the golden waist-cord and the ruby anklets sway at your waist and the single head-jewel dangles and swings — coming down from my lap, clap your hands upon the lap of the king of your cowherds! Clap, O wondrous one, clap your hands!

dp_2.translit:
Panmani muththu in pavaLam padhiththanna
enmani vaNNan ilangupoR Roottinmel
ninmani vaaymuth thilanganin nammaidhan
ammani melkottaay sappaani
aazhiyang kaiyane sappaani
dp_2.meaning: O my sapphire-hued one, like many gems, pearls and sweet coral inlaid together — while the pearls of your jewel-mouth gleam upon your shining golden ear-ornaments, clap your hands upon your mother's jewel! Clap, O you of the discus-bearing lovely hand, clap your hands!

dp_2.translit:
Thoonilaa muttraththe pondhu viLaiyaada
vaanilaa ambulee sandhiraa vaavendru
neenilaa ninpuga zhaanindra aayartham
konilaa vakkottaay sappaani
kudandhaik kidandhaane sappaani
dp_2.meaning: As you come into the moonlit courtyard to play, calling 'O sky-moon, O Ambuli, O Chandra, come!' — while you, the moon of the cowherds who stand singing your praise, shine — clap your hands so the cowherd-king may rejoice! Clap, O you who recline at Kudanthai (Kumbakonam), clap your hands!

dp_2.translit:
Puttiyil serum puzhudhiyum koNduvandhu
atti yamukki agampuk kaRiyaame
chatti thayirum thadaavinil veNNeyumuN
pattikkan rekottaay sappaani
paRpanaa paakottaay sappaani
dp_2.meaning: Bringing in slush and dust on your little body, smearing and dabbing it about, and slipping into the house unseen to eat up the curd in the pot and the butter in the jar — clap your hands, O unruly little calf! Clap, O Padmanabha, clap your hands!

dp_2.translit:
Thaariththu nootruvar thandhaisol koLLaadhu
poruththu vandhu pugundhavar maNNaaLa
paariththa mannar padappanj chavarkkuandru
theruyththa kaigaLaal sappaani
devaki singame sappaani
dp_2.meaning: When the hundred Kauravas, heedless of their father's word, came warring and intent to rule the land — so that those arrayed kings might fall, for the sake of the five Pandavas you once drove the chariot. Clap those very hands! Clap, O lion-cub of Devaki, clap your hands!

dp_2.translit:
Parandhittu nindra padukadal thannai
irandhitta kaimmel eRidhirai modha
karandhittu nindra kadalaik kalanga
sarandhotta kaigaLaal sappaani
saarngavih kaiyane sappaani
dp_2.meaning: When the wide outspread sea, though entreated, dashed its rising waves upon your hand (the sea-god refusing to yield) — to make that concealing ocean churn in turmoil, you fitted an arrow to the Sarnga bow. Clap those hands! Clap, O you of the Sarnga-bow hand, clap your hands!

dp_2.translit:
Kurakkinath thaale kuraikadal thannai
nerukki aNaikatti neeLnee rilangai
arakkar aviya adugaNai yaale
nerukkiya kaigaLaal sappaani
nemiyang kaiyane sappaani
dp_2.meaning: With the host of monkeys you dammed and bridged the roaring sea, and with deadly arrows you pressed hard so that the demons of sea-girt Lanka perished. Clap those hands! Clap, O you of the discus-bearing hand, clap your hands!

dp_2.translit:
ALandhitta thooNai avandhatta aange
vaLarndhittu vaaLugirch chinga vuruvaay
uLandhot tiraNiyan oNmaar vakalam
piLandhitta kaigaLaal sappaani
peymulai yuNdaane sappaani
dp_2.meaning: Striking apart the appointed pillar, rising up there and then as the man-lion of sword-sharp claws, you reached into his heart and split open the broad gleaming chest of Hiranya — clap those hands! Clap, O you who drank the breast of the demoness Putana, clap your hands!

dp_2.translit:
Adaindhittu amarargaL aazhkadal thannai
midaindhittu mandharam maththaaga naatti
vadamsutri vaasuki vankayi Raakk
kadaindhitta kaigaLaal sappaani
kaarmugil vaNNane sappaani
dp_2.meaning: When the celestials came to you, you planted Mount Mandara as the churning-rod in the deep sea, wound Vasuki as the strong rope, and churned it — clap those hands! Clap, O dark-cloud-hued one, clap your hands!

dp_2.translit:
Aatkollath thondriya aayarthang kovinai
naatkamazh poompozhil villiput thoorppattan
vetkaiyi naal sonna sappaani eeraindhum
vetkaiyi naalsollu vaarvinai pome
dp_2.meaning: These ten 'sappani' verses, sung with loving longing by the lord of Villiputhur of day-fragrant flower-groves, on the king of the cowherds who appeared to take us into his service — the karma of those who recite them with longing shall depart.

dp_2.translit:
Thodarsang kiligai salaarpilaa rennath thoonguponmaNi olippa
padumum madhappunal sora vaaraNam paiyanindru oorvadhupol
udangoodik kiNkiNi yaaravaa rippa udaimaNi paRaikaranga
thadandhaa LiNaikoNdu saaranga paaNi thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: As the linked chain jangles 'salaar-pilaar' and the hanging golden bells chime — like a rutting elephant moving slowly with its three-fold ichor flowing — with his anklets all jingling together and his waist-bells drumming, on his two broad feet, will the Sarnga-bearer toddle his faltering little walk?

dp_2.translit:
Sekka ridainunik kombil thondrum siRupiRai muLaipola
nakka senduvar vaayththiNNai meedhe naLirveNpal muLaiyilaga
akku vadamuduththu aamaith thaali pooNda anandha sayanan
thakka maamaNi vaNNan vaasudevan thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: Like a slender new crescent on the tip of a bough amid the red twilight, cool white milk-teeth gleam on the ledge of his moist red lips; wearing a string of conch-beads and a tortoise-shaped pendant — Ananta-sayana, the fitting great-sapphire-hued Vasudeva, will he toddle his faltering walk?

dp_2.translit:
Minnuk kodiyum orveN thingaLum soozhpari vedamumaay
pinnal thulangum arasilaiyum peethagach chittraadai yodum
minnil polindha thorkaar mugilpolak kazhuththinil kaaRai yodum
thannil polindha irudee kesan thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: Like a lightning-streak, a lone white moon and a surrounding halo; with a shining plaited pipal-leaf ornament and a little yellow silk garment; like a dark cloud lit by lightning, a collar at his throat — Hrishikesha, resplendent in himself, will he toddle his faltering walk?

dp_2.translit:
Kannar kudamthiRan thaaloth thoorik kaNagaNa siriththuvandhu
munvandhu nindru muththam tharumen mugilvaNNan thirumaarvan
thannaip petreRkuth thanvaa yamudham thandhu ennaith thaLirppikkindraan
thannetru maatralar thalaigaL meedhe thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: Welling over as if a pot of sugarcane-syrup were opened, laughing 'kana-kana', my cloud-hued one of the sacred chest comes, stands before me, and gives a kiss; giving me — who bore him — the nectar of his own mouth, he makes me blossom anew. Upon the heads of his vanquished foes, will he toddle his faltering walk?

dp_2.translit:
Munnalor veLLip perumalaik kuttan modumodu viraindhoda
pinnaith thodarndhathor karumalaik kuttan peyarndhadi yiduvadhupol
panni yulagam paraviyo vaappugazhp paladhe vanennum
thannambi yodap pinkoodach chelvaan thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: As if a calf of a great silver mountain rushed swiftly ahead and a calf of a dark mountain followed, shifting its feet behind — going after his elder brother Balarama, whom all the world praises endlessly, keeping up close behind, will he toddle his faltering walk?

dp_2.translit:
Orukaa lilsangu orukaalil sakkaram uLLadi poRiththamaindha
irukaa lumkoNdu angangu ezhudhinaaRpol ilachchinai padanadandhu
perugaa nindra inbaveL Laththinmel pinnaiyum peydhupeydhu
karugaarkkadalvaNNan kaamar thaadhai thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: With a conch marked on one sole and a discus on the other, his two feet impressing their stamp as though signing here and there — pouring wave upon wave onto the ever-swelling flood of bliss — the dark-ocean-hued one, the lovely Father of Manmatha, will he toddle his faltering walk?

dp_2.translit:
Padarpang kayamalar vaay negizhap panipadu siRudhuLipol
idangoN dasev vaayoori yoori itritru veezhanindru
kadunjek kazhuththin maNikkural pol udaimaNi kaNagaNena
thadandhaa LiNaikoNdu saarnga paaNi thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: As his spreading lotus-mouth loosens, like cool falling droplets the saliva of his red mouth wells and wells and drips down bit by bit; while his waist-bells ring 'kana-kana' like the deep gem-toned bell on a fierce ox's neck — on his two broad feet, will the Sarnga-bearer toddle his faltering walk?

dp_2.translit:
Pakkam karunjiRup paaRai meedhe aruvigaL pagarndhanaiya
akku vadamizhindh eRith thaazha aNiyalgul pudaipeyara
makka Lulaginil peydhaRi yaadha maNikkuzhavi yuruvin
thakka maamaNi vaNNan vaasudevan thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: Like cascades pouring down the side of a small dark rock, his string of conch-beads slips down and dangles, and his pretty waist sways from side to side; in the form of a jewel-like infant such as was never born among the people of the world, the fitting great-sapphire-hued Vasudeva, will he toddle his faltering walk?

dp_2.translit:
VeNpuzhudhi melpeydhu koNda aLaindhathor vezhaththin karungandrupol
theNpuzhudhi yaadith thirivik kiraman siRupugar padaviyarththu
oNpodhalar kamalach chiRukkaa luraiththu ondrum novaame
thaNpodhu koNda thavisin meedhe thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: Like a dark elephant-calf that has wallowed and heaped white dust upon itself, Trivikrama plays in the clear dust, faintly speckled and sweating; that his little blossom-lotus feet might not be hurt at all — upon a cool flower-strewn mat, will he toddle his faltering walk?

dp_2.translit:
Thiraineerch chandhira maNda lampol sengaNmaal kesavan than
thiruneer mugaththuth thulangu sutti thigazhndhengum pudaipeyara
peruneerth thiraiyezhu gangai yilum periyadhor theerthapalam
tharuneer siRuchchaNNam thuLLam sorath thaLarnadai nadavaano
dp_2.meaning: Like the moon's orb on the wave-tossed waters, the head-jewel gleaming on the sacred dewy face of red-eyed Tirumal, Kesava, shimmers and sways everywhere; as the trickling water of his little mouth — bestowing a sacred merit greater than the great wave-swelling Ganga — spills in droplets, will he toddle his faltering walk?

dp_2.translit:
Aayar kulaththinil vandhu thondriya anjana vaNNanthannai
thaayar magizha onnaar thaLarath thaLarnadai nadandhadhanai
veyar pugazhvittu siththan seeraal viriththana uraikkavallaar
maayan maNivaN Nanthaal paNiyum makkaLaip peruvaargaLe
dp_2.meaning: On the collyrium-hued one who appeared in the cowherds' clan, who toddled his faltering walk so his mothers rejoiced and his foes grew faint — those who can recite these verses, elaborated with excellence by Vishnuchittan whom the cowherds praise, shall obtain children who bow at the feet of the wondrous sapphire-hued Lord.

dp_2.translit:
Ponniyal kiNkiNi sutti puRamkatti
thanniya losai salanjala nendrida
minniyal megam viraindhedhir vandhaaRpol
ennidaikkottaraa achcho vachcho
emperu maanvaaraa achcho vachcho
dp_2.meaning: With golden anklets and a head-jewel and a band tied behind, their sound jingling 'salan-jalan' — like a lightning-laced cloud rushing swiftly to meet me — come tumbling into my arms, achcho, achcho (come, let me embrace you)! O my Lord, come, achcho, achcho!

dp_2.translit:
Sengama lappoovil thenuNNum vaNdepol
pangigaL vandhuun pavaLavaay moyppa
sanguvil vaaLthaNdu sakkara mendhiya
angaika laalevandhu achcho vachcho
aarath thazhuvaavandhu achcho vachcho
dp_2.meaning: As your curls come swarming over your coral mouth like bees sipping honey from a red lotus — with your lovely hands that bear conch, bow, sword, mace and discus, come, achcho, achcho! Come and embrace me close, achcho, achcho!

dp_2.translit:
Panjavar thoodhanaayp paaratham kaiseydhu
nanjumizh naagam kidandhanaR poygaipukku
anjap paNaththinmel paaynditt aruLseydha
anjana vaNNane achcho vachcho
aayar perumaane achcho vachcho
dp_2.meaning: O you who, as envoy of the five Pandavas, brought about the Bharata war; who entered the fair pool where the poison-spewing serpent lay and, leaping terribly upon its hoods, danced and showed your grace — O collyrium-hued one, come, achcho, achcho! O Lord of the cowherds, achcho, achcho!

dp_2.translit:
NaaRiya saandham namakkiRai nalgenna
theRi avaLum thiruvudam bilpoosa
ooriya kooninai uLLe yodungaandru
eRa vuruvinaay achcho vachcho
emperu maanvaaraa achcho vachcho
dp_2.meaning: When the hunchbacked woman (Kubja) was asked, 'Give us a little of your fragrant sandal-paste,' and she gladly smeared it on your sacred body — O you who that day straightened her, drawing her swollen hump inward so she rose erect — come, achcho, achcho! O my Lord, come, achcho, achcho!

dp_2.translit:
Kazhalmannar soozhak kadhirpol viLangi
ezhalutru meeNde irundhuunnai nokkum
suzhalaip peridhudaith thuchcho dhananai
azhala vizhiththaane achcho vachcho
aazhiyang kaiyane achcho vachcho
dp_2.meaning: Surrounded by anklet-wearing kings, shining like the sun — O you who burned with a fiery glance the deeply scheming, vain-natured foe who half-rose and sat down again, eyeing you — come, achcho, achcho! O you of the discus-bearing lovely hand, achcho, achcho!

dp_2.translit:
Porokkap paNniip poomip poRaitheerppaan
therokka voorndhaay sezhundhaar visayaRkaay
kaarokkum menik karumberung kaNNane
aarath thazhuvaavandhu achcho vachcho
aayargaL poreRe achcho vachcho
dp_2.meaning: To rid this earth of its burden, you waged the war and drove the chariot for the rich-garlanded Vijaya (Arjuna) — O great dark Krishna of cloud-like form — come and embrace me close, achcho, achcho! O bull among the cowherds, achcho, achcho!

dp_3.translit:
Anna vayal puthuvai aaNdaaL arangarkup
pannu thiruppaavai thiruppadhigam — innisaiyaal
paadik koduthaaNaR paamaalai poomaalai
soodik koduthaaLaich chol
dp_3.meaning: Speak of Andal of Puduvai (Srivilliputhur) of swan-filled fields, who composed for Lord Ranganatha the sacred Thiruppavai hymn, singing it in sweet melody as a garland of verse — she who also offered him the flower-garland she had first worn herself.

dp_3.translit:
Soodik koduththa sudark kodiye, tholpaavai
paadi aruLavalla pal vaLaiyaai —
naadi nee vengadavaRku ennai vidhi endra immaatram
naang kadavaa vaNName nalgu
dp_3.meaning: O radiant creeper-like one who gave the garland after wearing it, O many-bangled lady able to sing the ancient Pavai! Grant that we may never transgress the words you spoke — 'Sought and decreed me to the Lord of Venkatam.'

dp_3.translit:
Maargazhith thingaL madhiniRaindha nannaaLaal
neeraadap podhuveer podhumino nerizhaiyeer
seermalgum aaypaadich chelvach chiRumeergaaL
koorvel kodunthozhilan nandhagopan kumaran
eraarndha kaNNi yasodhai iLamsingam
kaarmeni sengaN kadhirmadhiyam polmugaththaan
naaraayaNane namakke paRai tharuvaan
paaror pugazhap padindhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: In the month of Margazhi, on this fine full-moon day, come, all who wish to bathe! O richly adorned, prosperous young girls of flourishing Gokulam — Narayana himself, the son of Nandagopa of the sharp spear and fearsome deeds, the young lion of lovely-eyed Yashoda, dark-hued, red-eyed, his face like the radiant moon, will grant us our boon. Come and undertake the vow, that all the world may praise us, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Vaiyaththu vaazhveergaaL naamumnam paavaikkuch
cheyyum girisaigaL keLeero paaRkadaluL
paiyath thuyindra paramanadipaadi
neyyuNNom paaluNNom naatkaale neeraadi
maiyittu ezhudhom malarittu naammudiyom
seyyaa thanaseyyom theekkuRaLaich chendrodhom
aiyamum pichchaiyum aandhanaiyum kaigaatti
uyyumaaR eNNi ugandhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O you who dwell in this world, hear the observances we keep for our Paavai vow. Singing the feet of the Supreme who gently reclines on the ocean of milk, we shall not eat ghee or milk; we shall bathe at dawn, wear no collyrium, deck our hair with no flowers; we shall do no forbidden deed, speak no cruel slander, but give alms and charity freely — thinking only of our deliverance, let us rejoice, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Ongi ulagaLandha uthaman perpaadi
naangaLnam paavaikkuch chaatrinee raadinaal
theenginri naadellaam thingaLmum maaraipeydhu
ongu peRumsen neloodu kayalugaLap
poonguvaLaip podhil poRivaNdu kaNpaduppath
thengaadhe pukkirundhu seerththa mulaipatri
vaanga kudam niRaikkum vaLLal perumpasukkaL
neengaadha selvam niRaindhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: If we sing the name of the Supreme One who grew tall and measured the worlds and bathe in dedication for our vow, then without any harm the whole land will have rain thrice a month. Amid the tall ripening red paddy the carp will leap, spotted bees will sleep in the kuvalai blossoms, and the great generous cows, their rich udders grasped, will fill the pots to the brim — undiminishing wealth will fill the land, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Aazhi mazhaikkaNNaa ondrunee kaigaravel
aazhiuL pukku mugandhukodu aarththueRi
oozhi mudhalvan uruvampol meykaRuthup
paazhiyam thoLudaip paRpanaaban kaiyil
aazhi polminni valampuri pol nindru
adhirndhu thaazhaadhe saarnga mudhaiththa saramazhaipol
vaazha ulaginil peydidaai naangaLum
maargazhi neeraada magizhndhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O rain-laden cloud, deep as the ocean, withhold nothing! Plunge into the sea, draw up its waters and rise roaring; grow dark like the form of the Lord, first cause of the aeons; flash like the discus in mighty-shouldered Padmanabha's hand, sound like his right-spiralled conch, and pour down without delay like the arrow-shower loosed from his Sarnga bow — so that the world may thrive and we too may joyfully bathe in Margazhi, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Maayanai mannu vadamadhurai maindhanaith
thooya peruneer yamunaith thuRaivanai
aayar kulaththinil thondrum aNiviLakkaith
thaayaik kudalviLakkam seydha thaamodharanaith
thooyomaay vandhunaam thoomalarthoo viththozhudhu
vaayinaal paadi manaththinaal sindhikkap
poya pizhaiyum pugudharuvaan nindranavum
theeyinil thoosaagum seppelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: The wondrous Lord, the youth of northern Mathura, the lord of the pure deep Yamuna's banks, the lovely lamp born in the cowherd clan, Damodara who brought lustre to his mother's womb — if we come pure, scatter fresh flowers, worship, sing with our lips and meditate with our minds, then our past sins and the wrongs yet to come will turn to ash in the fire. Declare this, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
PuLLum silambinakaaN puLLaraiyan koyilil
veLLai viLisangin perasvam kettilaiyo
piLLaai ezhundhiraai peymulai nanjuNdu
kaLLach chakadam kalakkazhiyak kaalochchi
veLLaththaravil thuyilamarndha viththinai
uLLaththuk koNdu munivargaLum yogigaLum
meLLa ezhundhang ariendra perasvam
uLLam pugundhu kuLirndhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: The birds are chirping — did you not hear, in the temple of the Lord of birds, the great blast of the white summoning conch? O girl, arise! The sages and yogis, holding in their hearts the Primal Seed who drank the poisoned breast of Putana, kicked over the deceitful cart, and reclines on the serpent in the flood, are gently rising and uttering 'Hari, Hari' — let that great sound enter and cool your heart. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Keesukeesu endruengum aanaichaaththan kalandhu
pesina pechcharavam kettilaiyo peyppeNNe
kaasum piRappum kalakalappak kaiperthu
vaasa naRumkuzhal aaychchiyar maththinaal
osai paduththa thayirasvam kettilaiyo
naayagap peNpiLLaai naaraayaNanmoorthi
kesavanaip paadavumnee kette kidaththiyo
desa mudaiyaai thiRavelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: Everywhere the bharadvaja birds chatter 'keechu keechu' — did you not hear that babble, O foolish girl? Did you not hear the sound of curd being churned by the fragrant-tressed cowherd women, their coins and ornaments jangling as their arms work the churn? O leader among us, even as we sing of Narayana the Lord, of Kesava, will you lie there merely listening? O radiant one, open the door, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Keezhvaanam veLLendru erumai siRuveedu
meyvaan parandhanakaaN mikkuLLa piLLaigaLum
povaanpo kindraaraip pogaamalkaaththuunnaik
koovuvaan vandhunindrom kodhugalam udaiya
paavaai ezhundhiraai paadip paRaikoNdu
maavaay piLandhaanai mallarai maattiya
devaadhi devanaich chendrunaam sevithaal
aavaavendru aaraayndhu aruLelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: The eastern sky is whitening; the buffaloes spread out to graze in the early morning. Holding back the other girls who were already setting off, we have come and stand calling you, O joyous maiden — arise! Singing, let us win our boon. If we go and worship the God of gods, who split the horse-demon's jaws and slew the wrestlers, he will graciously consider our wish and bless us. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Thoomani maadaththu suttrum viLakkeriyath
thoomam kamazhath thuyilaNaimel kaNvaLarum
maamaan magaLe maNikkadhavam thaazhthiRavaai
maameer avaLai ezhuppeero unmagaLthaan
oomaiyo andri sevido ananthalo
emap perundhuyil mandhirap pattaaLo
maamaayan maadhavan vaikundhan endrendru
naaman palavum navindrelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O daughter of my uncle, asleep on a soft bed in a jewelled mansion where lamps burn all around and incense wafts — open the gem-studded door! Aunt, will you not wake her? Is your daughter dumb, or deaf, or weary, or has she fallen under a charmed deep sleep? Chanting his many names — the great Mayan, Madhava, the Lord of Vaikuntha — let us wake her. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Notruch chuvarkkam puguginra ammanaai
maatramum thaaraaro vaasal thiRavaadhaar
naatrath thuzhaaymudi naaraayaNan nammaal
potrap paRaitharum puNNiyanaal
paNduorunaaL kootraththin vaayveezhndha
kumba karuNanum thotrum unakke
perundhuyilthaan thandhaano aatra
ananthal udaiyaai arungalame
thetramaay vandhu thiRavelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O lady who by your austerities are entering heaven — you who will not open the door, will you not even answer? Narayana of the fragrant tulasi-crown, the meritorious One whom we praise, grants the boon. Has even Kumbhakarna, who long ago fell into the jaws of death, surrendered his great sleep to you, that you slumber so deep? O rare jewel sunk in heavy sleep, come clearly awake and open the door, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Katruk kaRavaik kaNangaL palakaRandhu
settraar thiRalazhiyach chendru seruchcheyyum
kuttamondrillaadha kovalarththam porkodiye
putruaravu algul punamayile podharaai
suttraththu thozhimaar ellaarum vandhunin
muttram pugundhu mugilvaNNan perpaada
sittraadhe pesaadhe selvapeN daattinee
ettrukku uRangum poruLelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O golden creeper of the blameless cowherds who milk herds of young cows and march to battle to break their foes' strength — O forest-peahen with a slender waist, come forth! All your companions and friends have entered your courtyard, singing the name of the cloud-hued Lord. O prosperous girl, without stirring or speaking, what reason have you still to sleep? Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
KanaiththuiLam katrerumai kandrukku irangi
ninaiththu mulaivazhiye nindrupaal sora
nanaiththuillam seraakkum naRchelvan thangaai
paniththalai veezhanin vaasaR kadaipatrich
chinaththinaal thenilangaik komaanaich chetra
manaththukku iniyaanaip paadavumnee vaaythiRavaai
iniththaan ezhundhiraai eedhenna perurakkam
anaiththuillath thaarum aRindhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O younger sister of the rich cowherd whose young buffalo, lowing in tenderness for its calf, lets its milk flow until the house turns to slush — though we stand at your doorstep with dew falling on our heads, singing of the Lord dear to our hearts who in wrath destroyed the king of southern Lanka, you will not open your mouth. Wake now! What is this deep sleep? All the households know. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
PuLLinvaay keeNdaanaip pollaa arakkanaik
kiLLik kaLaindhaanaik keerththimai paadippoyp
piLLaigaL ellaarum paavaik kaLampukkaar
veLLi ezhundhu viyaazham uRangitru
puLLum silambinakaaN podharik kaNNinaai
kuLLak kuLirak kudaindhunee raadaadhe
paLLik kidaththiyo paavaainee nannaaLaal
kaLLam thavirndhu kalandhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: Singing the glory of him who split the crane-demon's beak and who plucked off and destroyed the wicked demon Ravana, all the other girls have already reached the place of our vow. Venus has risen, Jupiter has set, the birds are chirping — O doe-eyed girl, instead of bathing in the cool refreshing water, will you lie abed? On this auspicious day, give up your guile and join us. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
UngaL puzhakkadaith thottaththu vaaviyuL
sengazhuneer vaaynegizhndhu aambalvaay koombinakaaN
sengaR podikkoorai veNpal thavaththavar
thangaL thirukkoyil sangiduvaan podhandraar
engaLai munnam ezhuppuvaan vaaypesum
nangaai ezhundhiraai naaNaadhaai naavudaiyaai
sangodu sakkaram endhum thadakkaiyan
pangayak kaNNaanaip paadelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: In the pond of your backyard garden the red lily has opened and the white waterlily has folded — look! The ascetics in ochre robes with white teeth have gone to their temple to sound the conch. O lady who promised to wake us first — arise, you shameless one with the ready tongue! Let us sing of the lotus-eyed Lord whose broad hands bear the conch and discus. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Elle iLamkiLiye innam uRanguthiyo
silendru azhaiyenmin nangaiyeer podharugindren
vallaiun katturaigaL paNdeun vaayaRidhum
valleergaL neengaLe naanedhaan aayiduga
ollainee podhaai unakkenna verudaiyai
ellaarum pondhaaro pondhaarpondhu eNNikkoL
vallaanai kondraanai maatraarai maatrazhikka
vallaanai maayanaip paadelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: (Outside:) 'Hey, young parrot, are you still asleep?' (Inside:) 'Don't call so sharply, ladies; I am coming.' 'We know your glib words and your tongue of old.' 'You are the clever ones; let me be the foolish one.' 'Come quickly — what makes you so special? Have all arrived?' 'They have; come and count.' Let us sing of the wondrous Lord who slew the mighty elephant and can undo the strength of foes. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Naayaga naaynindra nandhago panudaiya
koyilkaap paane kodiththondrum
thoraNa vaayilkaap paane maNikkadhavam thaaLthiRavaai
aayar siRumiya romukku aRaipaRai
maayan maNivaNNan nennale vaaynerndhaan
thooyomaay vandhom thuyilezhap paaduva
vaayaalmun nammunnam maatraadhe ammaanee
neya nilaikkadhavam neekkelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O guardian of the temple of Nandagopa our chief, O keeper of the banner-flying ornamental gateway, draw back the bolt of the jewelled door! The wondrous sapphire-hued Lord promised us cowherd-girls our boon only yesterday. We have come pure to sing him awake, so do not refuse us at the very outset, good sir — open the lovingly-fastened door. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Amparame thaNNeere sore aRamseyyum
emperumaan nandhago paalaa ezhundhiraai
kombanaarkku ellaam kozhundhe kulaviLakke
emperu maatti yasodhaai aRivuRaai
amparam oodaaRuththu ongi ulaguaLandha
umbarko maane uRangaadhu ezhundhiraai
sempoR kazhaladich chelvaa baladhevaa
umbiyum neeyun uRangelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O Nandagopa our lord, who give cloth, water, and food in charity — arise! O Yashoda our lady, tender shoot and lamp of the clan of graceful women — awake! O Lord of the celestials who pierced the sky and grew to measure the worlds — sleep not, arise! O prosperous Baladeva of golden anklets — may you and your brother not sleep. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Undhu madhagaLitran odaadha thoLvaliyan
nandhago paalan marumagaLe nappinnaai
kandham kamazhum kuzhali kadaithiRavaai
vandhuengum kozhi azhaiththanakaaN maadhavip
pandhalmel palkaal kuyilinangaL koovinakaaN
pandhuaar viraliun maiththunan perpaadach
chendhaa maraikkaiyaal seeraar vaLaiolippa
vandhu thiRavaai magizhndhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O Nappinnai, daughter-in-law of Nandagopa — lord of rutting elephants and unyielding strength — O fragrant-tressed one, open the door! Everywhere the cocks are crowing; on the jasmine bower the cuckoo-flocks call again and again. O lady with a ball in your flower-soft fingers, that we may sing your husband's name, come with your lotus-red hands, your fine bangles jingling, and joyfully open the door. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Kuththu viLakkeriya kottukkaal kattilmel
meththendra panja sayanaththin meleRik
koththalar poongkuzhal nappinai kongaimel
vaiththuk kidandha malarmaarbaa vaaythiRavaai
maiththadam kaNNinaai neeun maNaaLanai
eththanai podhum thuyilezha ottaaykaaN
eththanaiyelum pirivuaatra killaayaal
thaththuvam andru thagavelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O broad, flower-garlanded Lord who reclines on the soft five-fold bed upon the legged cot, where the standing lamp burns, your head upon the breast of flower-tressed Nappinnai — speak to us! And you, dark wide-eyed Nappinnai, will not let your consort wake even a moment, unable to bear the briefest parting from him — that is neither right nor seemly. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Muppaththu moovar amararkku munsendru
kappam thavirkkum kaliye thuyilezhaai
seppam udaiyaai thiRaludaiyaai settraarkku
veppam kodukkum vimalaa thuyilezhaai
seppenna menmulaich chevvaaych chiRumarungul
nappinai nangaai thiruve thuyilezhaai
ukkamum thattoLiyum thandhuun maNaaLanai
ippodhe emmai neeraattelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O valiant One who goes before the thirty-three crore gods and removes their fear — awake! O upright, mighty, flawless One who brings dread to your foes — awake! O lovely Nappinnai of soft cup-like breasts, red lips, and slender waist — Goddess Lakshmi herself — awake! Give us the fan and the mirror for the rite, and let your consort have us bathe even now (grant us your grace). Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Etra kalangaL ethirpongi meedhaLippa
maatraadhe paalsoriyum vaLLal perumpasukkaL
aatrap padaiththaan magane aRivuRaai
ootram udaiyaai periyaai ulaginil
thotramaay nindra sudare thuyilezhaai
maatraar unakku valitholaindhuun vaasaRkaN
aatraadhu vandhuun adipaNiyu maapole
potriyaam vandhom pugazhndhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O son of him who owns the great generous cows that pour milk ceaselessly till the vessels brim and overflow — awake! O steadfast, great One, the radiance that appeared in this world — awake! Just as your foes, their strength broken, come helpless to your door and bow at your feet, so we too have come praising you. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
AmkaNmaa njaalaththu arasar abhimaana
pangamaay vandhunin paLLikkat tiRkeezhe
sangam iruppaarpol vandhu thalaippeydhom
kinginivaaychcheydha thaamaraip poopole
sengaN siRuchchiRidhe emmel vizhiyaavo
thingaLum aadhith thiyanum ezhundhaaRpol
amkaN iraNdumkoNdu engaLmel nokkudhiyel
engaLmel saabam izhindhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: Like the kings of this wide beautiful world who, their pride humbled, came and gathered in assembly beneath your couch, we too have come and reached it. Will your red eyes, like a lotus with little bell-shaped buds, open upon us little by little? If you look upon us with those two beautiful eyes, like the moon and sun rising together, the curse upon us will fall away. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Maari malaimuzhainjil mannik kidandhuuRangum
seeriya singam aRivutruth theevizhiththu veri
mayirponga eppaadum perndhuudhaRi
moori nimirndhu muzhangip puRappattup
poorumaa polenee poovaippoo vaNNaaun
koyilnindru ingane pondharuLik koppudaiya
seeriya singaa sanaththuirundhu yaamvandha
kaariyam aaraayndhu aruLelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: As the majestic lion that has lain asleep through the rains in a mountain cave awakens, opens its fiery eyes, shakes its fragrant mane bristling on every side, stretches, rises, roars and strides forth — even so, O Lord hued like the kaaya flower, come forth from your temple, seat yourself upon the splendid lion-throne, and graciously examine the purpose for which we have come. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Andruiv ulagam aLandhaai adiporri
sendrangkuth thenilangai settraai thiRalporri
pondrach chakadam udhaiththaai pugazhporri
kandru kuNilaa veRindhaai kazhalporri
kundru kudaiyaai eduththaai guNamporri
vendru pagaikedukkum ninkaiyil velporri
endrendrun sevagame eththip paRaikoLvaan
indruyaam vandhom irangelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: Praise to the feet that once measured these worlds; praise to the might that went and destroyed southern Lanka; praise to the fame that kicked the cart-demon to death; praise to the anklets that flung the calf as a stick at the wood-apple demon; praise to the grace that raised the mountain as an umbrella; praise to the spear in your hand that conquers and destroys foes. Praising your heroic deeds again and again, we have come today to receive our boon — be gracious. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Oruththi maganaayp piRandhuoor iravil
oruththi maganaay oLiththu vaLarath
tharikkilaan aagiththaan theengu ninaindha
karuththaip pizhaippiththuk kanjan vayitril
neruppenna nindra nedumaale unnai
aruththiththu vandhom paRaitharudhi yaagil
thiruththakka selvamum sevagamum yaampaadi
varuththamum theerndhu magizhndhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O great Lord who, born one night as the son of one woman (Devaki), grew up hidden as the son of another (Yashoda) — and who, when Kamsa could not bear it and plotted evil, foiled his design and stood like fire in his belly! We have come longing for you. If you grant us the boon, we shall sing your sacred wealth and heroism, our sorrow will end, and we shall rejoice. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Maale maNivaNNaa maargazhinee raaduvaan
melaiyaar seyvanagaL veNduvana kettiyel
njaalaththai ellaam nadunga muralvana
paalanna vaNNaththuun paancha sanniyame
polvana sangangaL poyppaa dudaiyanave
saalap perumpaRaiye pallaaNdu isaippaare
kola viLakke kodiye vidhaname
aalin ilaiyaai aruLelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O Tirumal, sapphire-hued Lord! If you would hear what we need to perform the Margazhi bathing-rite as the ancients did: conches like your milk-white Panchajanya that roar to make all the earth tremble; great resounding drums; singers to chant 'Pallandu'; beautiful lamps, banners, and a canopy. O Lord who floated on the banyan leaf in the deluge, grant us these. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Koodaarai vellumseer govindhaa undhannaip
paadip paRaikoNdu yaamperum sammaanam
naadu pugazhum parisinaal nandraagach
choodagame thoLvaLaiye thode sevippoove
paadagame endranaiya palagalanum yaamaNivom
aadai uduppom adhanpinne paaRchoru
moodaney peydhu muzhangai vazhivaarak
koodi irundhu kuLirndhelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: O Govinda, whose glory conquers all who oppose you! Singing your praise and receiving our boon, the honour we shall win — by which the whole land will praise us — is this: we shall adorn ourselves with bangles, armlets, earrings, ear-flowers, anklets and many such ornaments, and wear fine garments; and then, seated together, we shall feast on milk-rice drenched in ghee that drips down to our elbows, and be content. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
KaRavaigaL pinsendru kaanamserndhu uNpom
aRivuondrum illaadha aaykkulaththu undhannaip
piRavi peRundhanaip puNNiyam yaamudaiyom
kuRaiondrum illaadha govindhaa undhannodu
uRavel namakkuingu ozhikka ozhiyaadhu aRiyaadha
piLLaigaLom anbinaal undhannai
siRuper azhaiththanamum seeRi aruLaadhe
iRaivaanee thaaraay paRaiyelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: We follow the cattle, roam the forest, and eat there; we are of the ignorant cowherd clan — yet we have the great merit that you were born among us. O flawless Govinda, the bond between you and us here can never be severed. We are unknowing children; if out of love we have called you by small familiar names, do not be angry — O Lord, grant us our boon. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Sittram siRukaale vandhuunnai sevithuun
portaa maraiadiye porrum poruLkeLaai
pettrammeyththu uNNum kulaththil piRandhunee
kuttruEval engaLaik koLLaamal pogaadhu
ittraip paRaikoLvaan andrukaaN govindhaa
ettraikkum ezhezh piRavikkum unthannodu
uttrome aavom unakkenaam aatcheyvom
matrainam kaamangaL maatrelor empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: Hear, O Lord, the meaning of our coming in the small hours to worship and praise your golden lotus feet: born as you were in the clan that lives by grazing cattle, you cannot but accept our intimate service. It is not merely for today's boon that we have come, O Govinda — for all time and through seven-times-seven births we shall be bound to you alone and serve you alone. Turn away all our other desires. Arise, O our Paavai.

dp_3.translit:
Vangak kadalkadaindha maadhavanai kesavanai
thingaL thirumugaththu seyizhaiyaar sendruiRainji
angap paRaikoNda aatrai aNibhuduvaip
paingamalath thaNtheriyal pattarpiraan
kodhaisonna sangath thamizhmaalai muppadhum thappaame
inguip parisuraippaar eeriraNdu maalvaraithoL
sengaN thirumugaththuch chelvath thirumaalaal
engum thiruvaruLpetru inbuRuvar empaavaai
dp_3.meaning: This garland of thirty classical Tamil verses was sung by Kodai (Andal) — daughter of Pattarpiran (Periyalwar) of beautiful Puduvai, wearer of the cool fresh-lotus garland — telling how the jewelled, moon-faced maidens went, worshipped Madhava, Kesava who churned the ship-bearing ocean, and obtained their boon. Those who recite all thirty here without fault shall everywhere receive the grace of the prosperous Tirumal — of the four mountain-like shoulders and the red-eyed lovely face — and live in joy. O our Paavai.

dp_9.translit:
Kaattave kaNda paadha kamalam nallaadai undhi,
thettarum udhara bandham thirumaarbu kaNdam sevvaai,
vaattamil kaNgaL meni muniyeRith thani pugundhu,
paattinaal kaNdu vaazhum paaNar thaaL paravinome
dp_9.meaning: We bow to the feet of Thiruppaan Alwar, who — carried into the shrine on the sage's shoulders — beheld, one by one as the Lord revealed them, his lotus feet, fine garment, navel, the rare waist-band, sacred chest, throat, red lips, and tireless eyes and form, and who lived blessed, singing them in song.

dp_9.translit:
Amalan aadhipiraan adiyaarkku ennai aatpaduththa
vimalan viNNavar kon viraiyaar pozhil vengadavan
nimalan ninmalan needhi vaanavan neeL madhiL arangaththammaan thiruk
kamala paadham vandhu en kaNNinuLLana okkindrathe
dp_9.meaning: The flawless primal Lord who made me a servant of his devotees; the pure One, king of the celestials, Lord of fragrant-groved Venkatam; the stainless, immaculate, righteous God — the Lord of Srirangam of long ramparts: his sacred lotus feet have come and fixed themselves within my eyes.

dp_9.translit:
Uvandha uLLaththanaay ulagam aLandhu aNdamuRa
nivandha neeLmudiyan andru nerndha nisaasararai
kavarndha vengaNaik kaakuththan kadiyaar pozhil arangaththammaan araich
chivandha aadaiyin mel sendrathu aamen sindhanaiye
dp_9.meaning: He of the tall crown who, with a glad heart, measured the worlds until they reached the cosmic shell; Kakutstha (Rama) whose fierce arrows once seized the night-roving demons — the Lord of Srirangam of fragrant groves: upon the red garment about his waist my thought has gone and rests.

dp_9.translit:
Mandhi paay vada vengada maamalai vaanavargaL
sandhi seyya nindraan arangath aravin aNaiyaan
andhi pol niRath aadaiyum adhanmel ayanaip padaiththa or ezhil
undhi melatha andro adiyen uLLath innuyire
dp_9.meaning: He who stands on the great northern hill of Venkatam, where monkeys leap and the celestials worship at twilight; he who reclines on the serpent at Srirangam — upon his twilight-hued garment, and above it the one beautiful navel that bore Brahma the creator, there rests the very life within this servant's heart.

dp_9.translit:
Sadhuramaa madhiL soozh ilangaikku iRaivan thalaipaththu
udhira ottiyor vengaNai uyththavan odha vaNNan
madhuramaa vaNdu paada maamayil aada arangaththammaan thiruvayitru
dhara bandhanam en uLLaththuL nindru ulaagindrathe
dp_9.meaning: The ocean-hued Lord who drove a single fierce arrow to make the ten heads of the king of square-rampart Lanka fall and bleed — the Lord of Srirangam where sweet bees hum and great peacocks dance: the sacred band about his belly stands within my heart and moves there.

dp_9.translit:
Paaramaaya pazhavinai patraRuththu ennaiththan
vaaramaakki vaiththaan vaiththathandri en uL pugundhaan
kora maadhavam seydhanankol aRiyen arangaththammaan thiru
vaara maarbu andro adiyenai aatkoNdathe
dp_9.meaning: He severed the bond of my burdensome old karma, made me wholly his, and not stopping at that, entered into me. What dread austerity did I perform to deserve this? I do not know. It was the sacred, garland-adorned chest of the Lord of Srirangam that took this servant into his service.

dp_9.translit:
ThuNda veNpiRaiyaan thuyar theerththavan anjiRaiya
vaNdu vaazh pozhil soozh aranganagar meya appan
aNdar aNda pahiraNdaththu oru maanilam ezhumaalvarai mutrum
uNda kaNdam kaNdeer adiyenai uyyakkoNdathe
dp_9.meaning: He who removed the sorrow of the bearer of the white crescent (Shiva); the Father enshrined in Srirangam, ringed by groves where fine-winged bees dwell — behold, it was the very throat that swallowed the one great earth, the seven mountains, and all the cosmic spheres within their shell, that uplifted and saved this servant.

dp_9.translit:
Kaiyinaar surisangan alaazhiyar neeLvaraipol
meyyanaar thuLava viraiyaar kamazh neeLmudiyem
aiyanaar aNiyangananaar aravin aNaimisai meya maayanaar
seyya vaay aiyo ennaich chindhai kavarndhathuve
dp_9.meaning: He who holds the spiralled conch and blazing discus in his hands, whose form is vast as a great mountain; our Lord of the tall crown fragrant with tulasi; the wondrous One reclining on the serpent at beautiful Srirangam — alas, his red lips have stolen away my mind.

dp_9.translit:
Pariyanaaki vandha avuNan udal keeNda amararkku
