ariya aadhi piraan arangath amalan mugaththu
kariya vaakip pudaiparandhu miLirndhu sevvari odi neeNda ap
periya vaaya kaNgaL ennaip pedhaimai seydhanave
dp_9.meaning: The primal Lord, hard for even the gods to attain, who tore open the body of the demon (Hiranya) that came in monstrous form; the flawless One of Srirangam — those great eyes upon his face, dark, broad-spreading, glittering, with long red lines running through them, have made me lose myself in wonder.

dp_9.translit:
Aalamaamaraththin ilaimel oru baalakanaay
njaalam ezhum uNdaan arangath aravin aNaiyaan
kolamaa maNi aaramum muththuth thaamamum mudivilla thor ezhil
neela meni aiyo niRai koNdathen nenjinaiye
dp_9.meaning: He who, as a little child upon a banyan leaf, swallowed the seven worlds; he who reclines on the serpent at Srirangam — his dark-blue form of boundless beauty, with splendid gem-strings and pearl-garlands, alas, has captured all the fullness of my heart.

dp_9.translit:
KoNdal vaNNanaik kovalanaay veNNey
uNda vaayan en uLLam kavarndhaanai
aNdar konaNi aranganen amudhinaik
kaNda kaNgaL matrondrinaik kaaNaave
dp_9.meaning: The cloud-hued One, who as the cowherd ate the butter, whose mouth stole away my very heart; the king of the celestials, the Lord of beautiful Srirangam, my nectar — the eyes that have beheld him will never look upon anything else.

dp_8.translit:
MaNdangudi enbar maamaRaiyor, manniyaseerth
thoNdaradippodi thonnagaram, vaNdu
thiNarththa vayal thennarangaththu ammaanaip, paLLi
uNarththum piraanudhiththa voor
dp_8.meaning: The great Vedic scholars call it Mandangudi — the ancient town of the ever-glorious Thondaradippodi, the birthplace of the saint who awakens from his sacred sleep the Lord of southern Srirangam, girt by bee-dense fields.

dp_8.translit:
Kadhiravan kuNadhisaich chigaram vandhu aNaindhaan
kanaviruL agandrathu kaalaiyam pozhudhaay
madhu virindhu ozhuginа maamalar ellaam
vaanavar arasargaL vandhuvandhu eeNdi
edhirdhisai niRaindhanar ivarodum pugundha
irungaLitru eettamum pidiyodu murasum
adhirdhalil alaikadal pondrulathu engum
arangaththammaa! paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: The sun has reached the peak of the eastern quarter; the dense darkness has fled and it is now early dawn. All the great flowers spread their nectar; the kings of the celestials throng in, wave upon wave, filling the quarter opposite; with them enter great herds of bull-elephants with their cows, and the drums sound everywhere like the roaring, surging sea. O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_8.translit:
Kozhungodi mullaiyin kozhumalar aNavik
koorndhathu kuNadhisai maarudam idhuvo
ezhundhana malaraNaip paLLikoL annam
eenpani nanaindhatham irunjiRagu udhaRi
vizhungiya mudhalaiyin pilampurai pezhvaay
veLLeyiRu uRavadhan vidaththanukku anungi
azhungiya aanaiyin arundhuyar keduththa
arangaththammaa! paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: Is this the eastern breeze, grown strong as it caresses the rich blossoms of the lush jasmine creeper? The swans that slept on their flower-beds rise, shaking the cold dew from their broad wings. O Lord who relieved the grievous agony of the elephant Gajendra, tormented by the venom of the crocodile's cavern-like gaping jaws and white fangs — O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_8.translit:
Sudaroli parandhana soozhdhisai ellaam
thunniya thaaragai minnoli surungi
padaroli pasuththanan panimadhi ivano
paayiruL agandrathu paimpozhil kamugin
madalidaik keeRi vaN paaLaigaL naaRa
vaigaRai koorndhathu maarudam idhuvo
adaloli thigazhtharu thigiri am thadakkai
arangaththammaa! paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: Radiant light has spread across all the encircling quarters; the thick-clustered stars dim their twinkling; the cool moon has grown pale; the wide darkness is gone. Splitting through the sheaths of the areca palms in the green groves, their rich spathes give off fragrance — is this the dawn breeze grown full? O Lord whose broad beautiful hand bears the discus blazing with warlike splendour — O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_8.translit:
Meettila medhigaL thaLaividu maayargaL
veynguzhal osaiyum vidaimaNik kuralum
eettiya isaidhisai parandhana vayaluL
irundhina surumbinam ilangaiyar kulaththai
vaattiya varisilai vaanavar eRe
maamuni veLviyaik kaaththu avabiradham
aattiya aduthiRal ayoththiyem arase
arangaththammaa! paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: On the high ground the cowherds loose the young buffaloes from their tethers; the sound of their bamboo flutes and the bells of the bulls gather into music spreading in every direction; in the fields the swarms of bees settle. O bull among the celestials, of the lined bow that destroyed the clan of the Lankans; O victorious king of Ayodhya, who guarded the great sage's sacrifice and performed the avabhrtha bath — O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_8.translit:
Pulambina putkaLum poompozhilgaLin vaay
poyitruk kangul pugundhathu pulari
kalandhathu kuNadhisai kanaikadal aravam
kaLivaNdu mizhatriya kalambagam punaindha
alangalan thodaiyalkoNdu adiyiNai paNivaan
amarargaL pugundhanar aadhalil ammaa
ilangaiyar kon vazhipaadu sei koyil
emberumaan! paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: The birds in the flowering groves are calling; the night has passed and dawn has come. In the eastern quarter mingles the roar of the resounding sea. Bearing fragrant mixed-flower wreaths abuzz with joyful bees, the celestials have come to bow at your two feet, O Lord. O our God, enshrined in the temple where the king of Lanka (Vibhishana) offers worship — O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_8.translit:
Iraviyar maNi nedunthe rodum ivaro
iRaiyavar padhinoru vidaiyarum ivaro
maruviya mayilinan aRumugan ivano
marudharum vasukkaLum vandhuvandhu eeNdi
puraviyodu aadalum paadalum therum
kumaradaNdam pugundhu eeNdiya veLLam
aruvarai anaiyanin koyilmun nivaro
arangaththammaa! paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: Here come the twelve Adityas with their tall jewelled chariots; here the eleven Rudras upon their bulls; here the six-faced Murugan on his peacock; the Maruts and the Vasus throng in, wave upon wave — with horses, dancing, singing, chariots, and the deva-host surging like a flood before your temple, vast as a mighty mountain. O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_8.translit:
Andharath amarargaL koottangaL ivaiyo
arundhava munivarum marudharum ivaro
indhiran aanaiyum thaanum vandhu ivano
emberumaan un koyilin vaasal
sundharar nerukka vichchaadharar nookka
iyakkarum mayanginar thiruvadi thozhuvaan
andharam paaridam illai matru idhuvo
arangaththammaa! paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: Here are the assembled hosts of the sky-celestials; here the great ascetic sages and the Maruts; here Indra himself with his elephant — O our Lord, at the gateway of your temple the Gandharvas press in, the Vidyadharas push forward, and even the Yakshas are bewildered in the throng, all eager to worship your sacred feet. There is no room left in sky or on earth — see this crowd! O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_8.translit:
Vambavizh vaanavar vaayuRai vazhanga
maanidhi kapilai oNkaNNaadi mudhalaa
emberumaan padimakkalam kaaNdaRku
eRpana vaayina koNdu nan munivar
thumburu naaradar pugundhanar ivaro
thondrinan iraviyum thulangoli parappi
ambara thalaththil nindru agalgindrathu iruLpoy
arangaththammaa! paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: The celestials, fragrant flowers blooming, present their offerings; bringing the auspicious objects fit for the sacred service — the wish-granting cow Kapila, the bright mirror, and the rest — the good sages Tumburu and Narada have entered. The sun has appeared, spreading its glowing light; the darkness departs, withdrawing from the field of the sky. O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_8.translit:
Edhamil thaNNumai ekkam maththaLiye
yaazhkuzhal muzhavamod isaidhisai kezhumi
geethangaL paadinar kinnarar gerudargaL
kandharuvar avar kanguluL ellaam
maadhavar vaanavar saaraNar iyakkar
siddharum mayanginar thiruvadi thozhuvaan
aadhalil avarkku naaL olakkam aruLa
arangaththammaa! paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: The faultless drums, the ekkalam, the mridanga, the yazh, the flute and the great drum mingle their music in every direction; all through the night the Kinnaras, Garudas and Gandharvas have sung their songs; the great ascetics, celestials, Charanas, Yakshas and Siddhas stand bewildered in devotion, longing to worship your sacred feet. So, to grant them your daily audience — O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_8.translit:
Kadimalark kamalangaL malarndhana ivaiyo
kadhiravan kanaikadal muLaiththanan ivano
thudiyidaiyaar surikuzhal pizhindhu udhaRith
thugil uduththu eRinar soozhpunal arangaa
thodaiyoththa thuLavamum koodaiyum polindhu
thondriya thoL thoNdaradippodi ennum
adiyanai aliyan endru aruLi un adiyaarkku
aatpaduththaay paLLi ezhundharuLaaye
dp_8.meaning: See, the fragrant lotuses have blossomed; the sun has risen over the resounding sea. The slender-waisted women, wringing and shaking out their curly hair, don fresh garments and bathe, O Lord of Srirangam girt with waters. O Lord, graciously calling this servant 'Thondaradippodi' — he whose shoulders shine with well-strung tulasi and the flower-basket — declaring 'he is worthy of grace', and making him a servant of your devotees: O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred sleep!

dp_10.translit:
Verondrum naanaRiyen vedam thamizhseydha
maaRan sadagopan vaNkurukoor eRu, engaL
vaazhvaam endreththum madhurakaviyaar emmai
aaLvaar avare araN
dp_10.meaning: Madhurakavi, who praises none but Maran Satakopa (Nammalwar) of generous Kurugur — he who rendered the Veda into Tamil — saying 'He is our very life and refuge': that Madhurakavi himself is our master and our fortress.

dp_10.translit:
KaNNi nuN siRuth thaambinaal kattuNNap
paNNiya peru maayan en appanil
naNNith thenkurukoor nambi endrakkaal
aNNikkum amudhooRum en naavukke
dp_10.meaning: Sweeter even than my Father — the great wondrous One (Krishna) who let himself be bound by the slender little knotted cord — is this: when I draw near and merely utter 'Nambi of southern Kurugur' (Nammalwar), nectar wells up upon my tongue.

dp_10.translit:
Naavinaal navitru inbam eydhinen
mevinen avan ponnadi meymmaiye
devu matraRiyen kurukoor nambi
paavin innisai paadith thirivane
dp_10.meaning: By uttering his name with my tongue I have attained bliss; I have truly clung to his golden feet. I know no other god than Nambi of Kurugur (Nammalwar); I wander singing the sweet music of his verses.

dp_10.translit:
Thiridhandhaagilum deva piraanudai
kariya kolath thiru uruk kaaNban naan
periya vaN kurukoor nagar nambikkaaL
uriyanaay adiyen petra nanmaiye
dp_10.meaning: Even as I wander about, I behold the dark beautiful sacred form of the Lord, the God of gods — such is the good fortune this servant has gained by becoming the rightful servant of Nambi of great, generous Kurugur.

dp_10.translit:
Nanmaiyaal mikka naanmaRaiyaaLargaL
punmaiyaagak karudhuvar aadhalin
annaiyaay aththanaay ennai aaNdidum
thanmaiyaan sadagopan en nambiye
dp_10.meaning: Though the most virtuous masters of the four Vedas may regard it as lowly that I serve a man rather than God, Satakopa (Nammalwar) — my Nambi, who rules over me like both a mother and a father — is of such a nature that I cling to him still.

dp_10.translit:
Nambinen piRar nanporuL thannaiyum
nambinen madavaaraiyum munnellaam
sempon maadath thirukkurukoor nambikku
anbanaay adiyen sadhirththen indre
dp_10.meaning: In former days I set my trust upon others' goods and upon charming women. But this day I have grown wise: I have become a loving servant of Nambi of Tirukkurugur of golden mansions.

dp_10.translit:
Indru thottu ezhumaiyum empiraan
nindru than pugazh eththa aruLinaan
kundra maadath thirukkurukoor nambi endrum
ennai igazhvilan kaaNmine
dp_10.meaning: From this day and through all seven births, my Lord (Nammalwar) has graced me to stand praising his glory. Behold — Nambi of Tirukkurugur of hill-high mansions will never forsake me.

dp_10.translit:
KaNdu koNdu ennaik kaarimaaRap piraan
paNdai valvinai paatri aruLinaan
eNdisaiyum aRiya iyambugen
oNdamizhch chadagopan aruLaiye
dp_10.meaning: Lord Kari Maran (Nammalwar) sought me out, took me as his own, and graciously destroyed my old, stubborn karma. I shall proclaim, that all eight directions may know, the grace of Satakopa of the lustrous Tamil.

dp_10.translit:
AruLkoNdaadum adiyavar inbuRa
aruLinaan av arumaRaiyin poruL
aruLkoNdu aayiram in thamizh paadinaan
aruLkaNdeer iv vulaginil mikkathe
dp_10.meaning: That his devotees who cherish his grace might rejoice, he revealed the meaning of the rare Veda; out of grace he sang the thousand sweet Tamil verses (Thiruvaymozhi). Behold — it is grace alone that is supreme in this world.

dp_10.translit:
Mikka vediyar vedaththin utporuL
niRkap paadi en nenjuL niRuththinaan
thakka seerch chadagopan en nambikkaaL
pukka kaadhal adimaip payanandre
dp_10.meaning: He sang so that the inner meaning of the Veda of the great seers might stand firm, and he established it within my heart. This is the very fruit of the loving servitude into which I have entered for my Nambi, Satakopa of fitting glory.

dp_10.translit:
Payanandraagilum paangalaraagilum
seyalnandraagath thiruththip paNikoLvaan
kuyilnindru aarppozhil soozhkurukoor nambi
muyalgindren undran moykazhaRku anbaiye
dp_10.meaning: Even if I am profitless, even if I am unfit, he refines my conduct and takes me into his service. O Nambi of Kurugur, ringed by groves where cuckoos call ceaselessly — I strive only for love of your anklet-clad feet.

dp_10.translit:
Anban thannai adaindhavargatkellaam
anban thenkurukoor nagar nambikku
anbanaay madhurakavi sonnasol
nambuvaarpadhi vaikundham kaaNmine
dp_10.meaning: To all who take refuge in the loving Lord, he (Nammalwar) is loving; and as a loving servant of that Nambi of southern Kurugur, Madhurakavi has spoken these words. Behold — for those who hold them in faith, their abode shall be Vaikuntha.
